title: Tags, Signatures, Verification, and Release Identity · History Brief: Gita Tag Object Peeling
summary: A release tag moved, a lightweight tag shadowed a branch name, signatures use different formats, and an artifact claims a source revision that was never built. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita tag object peeling increment in release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish lightweight refs from annotated tag objects, nested tags, peeling, target types, messages, tagger identity, and replacement risk.
Define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Tag Object Peeling
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G41 is a theory in a disposable repository. the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review. The learner must predict verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy, run git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0, explain the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist, test this changed condition: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Tag Object Peeling contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish lightweight refs from annotated tag objects, nested tags, peeling, target types, messages, tagger identity, and replacement risk.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G41 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review. The learner must predict verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy, run git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0, explain the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist, test this changed condition: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest. A defensible solution applies Gita Tag Object Peeling, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 3
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review
git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0
# expected: verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy
# observation: the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist
# changed-case: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest
# transfer-limit: signature validity alone does not prove key ownership, authorization, build, or delivered bytes
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Tag Object Peeling evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-tag-object-peeling-1cpml8" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Tag Object Peeling into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-tag-object-peeling-1cpml8
# scenario-key: trace-1cpml8 probe-8lmpc1 boundary-pml81c owner-1cpml8x fault-1cpml8z
# before-ref: main=a11ce1c candidate=b00b1l8
# hypothesis: the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review
git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0
# expected: verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy
# observation: the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist
# changed-case: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce1c candidate=c0ffee8
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest
# transfer-limit: signature validity alone does not prove key ownership, authorization, build, or delivered bytes
